# searching-apple-notes

> A Claude Code skill that turns your Apple Notes archive into a semantically searchable second brain. You ask Claude in plain English or Chinese, it searches your notes locally with BGE-M3 embeddings, reads the relevant ones, and answers with citations — no API keys, no MCP setup, fully private.

This project lets users on macOS expose their Apple Notes to Claude Code via a drop-in skill. Search is semantic (BGE-M3 + ChromaDB), local-first (no data leaves the Mac), and bilingual (optimized for Chinese-English mixed content). The skill also lets Claude write new notes back into Apple Notes from a conversation, with native hashtag support.

The repository contains two ways to use the same underlying search engine: a Claude Code Skill (recommended, primary) and an MCP server (legacy, kept for Claude Desktop and Poke AI iMessage users).

## Primary docs

- [Main README](README.md): What the project is, how to install, two usage paths (Skill vs MCP), FAQ, dialogue examples in English and Chinese
- [Skill README](skills/searching-apple-notes/README.md): Detailed bilingual docs for the Claude Code skill — what it is, why it works, what it feels like to use, install, file layout, the four helper scripts, the native Apple Notes hashtag bridging trick, privacy guarantees
- [SKILL.md](skills/searching-apple-notes/SKILL.md): The actual instructions Claude Code reads when the skill activates — trigger conditions, available commands, search strategy, dialogue-to-note workflow

## Helper scripts (the skill's four tools)

- [vector_search.py](skills/searching-apple-notes/scripts/vector_search.py): Semantic vector search CLI with BGE-M3 + ChromaDB. Optional filters: tag, folder, date range. Returns JSON.
- [get_note.py](skills/searching-apple-notes/scripts/get_note.py): Fetch full plain-text content of a single note by Notes.app index when the search snippet isn't enough.
- [create_note.py](skills/searching-apple-notes/scripts/create_note.py): Create a new Apple Note from a markdown body via AppleScript. Hashtags written as #TagName become real Apple Notes hashtags.
- [sync_index.py](skills/searching-apple-notes/scripts/sync_index.py): One-shot pipeline — re-export Notes.app to SQLite, bridge native hashtags from NoteStore.sqlite, run incremental vector index update.

## How to install

The recommended path takes about 5 minutes:

1. Clone: `git clone https://github.com/yinanli1917-cloud/searching-apple-notes.git`
2. Install Python deps: `pip3 install -r requirements.txt`
3. One-time export: `python3.12 scripts/export_notes_fixed.py`
4. One-time index build: `python3.12 scripts/indexer.py` (downloads BGE-M3 ~2 GB)
5. Drop the skill into Claude Code: `cp -r skills/searching-apple-notes ~/.claude/skills/`
6. Restart Claude Code, ask it to find something in your notes

After install, you don't run any commands — you just talk to Claude.

## What this is NOT

- Not a SaaS product. No accounts, no servers, no subscriptions.
- Not a search box. Users never type queries directly — Claude handles that.
- Not a Notion/Obsidian/Evernote tool. Apple Notes only (PRs welcome for other backends).
- Not OpenAI-dependent. Uses local BGE-M3, not text-embedding-3-large.
- Not an MCP-first project anymore. The MCP server is preserved for legacy users but new development is in the Skill.

## Keywords for discovery
